answer:
As a new student at Kennesaw State University, you have the opportunity not only to pursue your academic goals but also to build relationships and immerse yourself in community life through housing and dining options available on-campus. Here's what you should know:

Living on Campus - Housing Options for Freshmen: Kennesaw State University offers a variety of living arrangements designed to cater to different needs, preferences, and budgets. As a freshman, I would recommend exploring the residence halls that are specifically designated as housing options for first-year students or sophomores who have not yet reached their junior year in high school. These residences provide an environment where you can meet other incoming freshmen like yourself while also having access to supportive resources and services tailored to your needs, such as academic advising, career counseling, health care facilities, and more.

Living on Campus - Benefits: Living in a residence hall or dormitory provides an opportunity for you to be physically close to classmates with similar interests and backgrounds while also fostering relationships that can last throughout your university experience. It's essential to find ways to connect, whether through shared activities like attending events on campus, joining clubs related to your academic pursuits or simply striking up conversations in common areas during meal times.

Eating on Campus - Dining Options: Kennesaw State University offers a diverse range of dining facilities across both campuses that cater to various dietary preferences and restrictions while also providing an opportunity for social interaction among students, faculty, staff, and guests from different backgrounds. The university's commitment to quality food is evident in the compliments received by many students about their culinary experiences on campus - such as Zachery Harris who praised lemon pepper wings or Leah Olds with her positive review of a friendly dining hall employee named Mary, whose exceptional service made for an enjoyable experience.

KSU Dining Services also offers group-dining options to accommodate large groups and special events on campus like Homecoming weekend when students gather in the commons area or during school trips where they might visit other campuses as part of their educational journey at Kennesaw State University. 

As a freshman, I encourage you to explore these

sources.Title: Housing and Residence Life
sources.Text: More Than a Place to Call Home Living on-campus at Kennesaw State University is about more than having a bed on campus—it's about building relationships and finding a community where you belong. Living on campus has helped me find myself, both by being in close proximity to other people my age and being able to attend events on campus. Key’Shawn Senior I chose to live on campus because I wanted to have an experience that would teach me something different from my small-town lifestyle. Living on campus has given me more opportunities to interact with other students on campus. Christopher Senior Living on campus, I always have a support system of people who are going through or struggling with the same classes I am. I also feel more connected to the campus. Alexis Sophomore

sources.Title: Discover Student Affairs - Kennesaw State University
sources.Text: Resources Strategic Planning, Assessment, and Analysis (SPAA) seeks to establish a supportive atmosphere that nurtures an assessment-oriented culture across the Division, guiding an evaluation of student learning experiences beyond traditional classroom settings. SPAA assumes the role of a guiding force through training and advisory services, fostering leadership and endeavors to fulfill both divisional and university missions by employing crafted assessment methodologies. learn more At Kennesaw State, our many Campus Services departments work together to provide the essentials of campus life along with exceptional customer service to the University’s students, faculty, staff and visiting guests. Parent and Family Programs Parent and Family Programs provides resources that support parents, families, and supporters of KSU students, ensuring a successful transition to college life by helping them stay connected to and involved with their KSU Owl. learn more Kennesaw State acknowledges and upholds the First Amendment rights of every individual, encompassing the liberties of free speech and peaceful assembly. Furthermore, KSU is fully aware of its duty to establish a safe educational experience that permits enrolled students and staff members to articulate their perspectives without impeding the functioning of the University. Learn more about your freedom of expression and engaging in expressive activities on both Kennesaw State campuses. learn more Questions, Concerns, & Reporting The Student Affairs team is dedicated to offering our support and assistance in any way possible. As a KSU student, you may encounter concerns or issues with academic, campus, vendor or enrollment services matters. submit a concern For all official complaints, we kindly request that you submit them using the form above. Additionally, if you encounter a person of concern, please submit a Red Flag Report to help prevent any future crises. learn more Scrappy's Bucket List Finding new ways to get involved on campus is easy with Scrappy's Bucket List (SBL)! All currently enrolled students at KSU are eligible to participate in this ongoing challenge, which can be completed at any time. All you need to do is complete 63 items on Scrappy's Bucket List before you graduate. See the list At Kennesaw State, traditions and spirit tell our shared story to bring us together. Through events such as Weeks of Welcome, Scrappy Hour, Homecoming, and KSU Day, students learn about what connects us as a campus community and deepen their sense of belonging and connection. learn more Student Affairs is dedicated to helping your growth and development. Our

sources.Title: University Dining
sources.Text: More Than Just a Meal Dining at Kennesaw State University is about more than just food on your plate. It’s about connection, convenience, and creating an experience that supports you throughout your day on campus. Please keep the lemon pepper wings on Fridays. They were easily the best wings I've ever had by a long shot, and I think I speak on behalf of many other people when I say we want this flavor to stay. Zachery Harris Student Please pass along our sincere compliments to your culinary, service, and front-of-house staff—students raved about the food, and everyone was so impressed with the management of the commons (and we know how hectic it gets in there with large groups). I just want to reiterate that we love working with you all, and we really appreciate your help in feeding these large groups! Also, with the one-off school groups that have been visiting this week, everything has been absolutely seamless. We can’t thank you enough for your help and hospitality! Jessica Reaves Group-Dining Visitor I want to give a great review of the kind staff member named Mary who was working the sandwich line all by herself. Despite the long line of 10 people, she was exceptionally friendly, greeting every individual with a pleasant smile and a welcoming hello. She also executed her job well, using a clean knife and cutting up the ingredients for sandwiches precisely. Mary is so hard-working and really adds to my overall great experience at KSU. Leah Olds Student

sources.Title: Undergraduate Admissions
sources.Text: APPLY FOR FREE Who will you become at KSU? Kennesaw State University empowers students to seek, strive and succeed in academics and beyond. In addition to offering more than 190 degree and certificate programs in 11 colleges, KSU leads in innovative teaching and learning and boasts two beautiful campuses with nearly 48,000 students. Owl Nation is home to vibrant campus cultures, close enough to Atlanta for students to explore exciting events, fun activities, and coveted career opportunities. We’re excited that you’ve chosen KSU as your academic doorway to reach your future potential and purpose. Our number one priority at KSU is our students and their success. KSU offers majors in STEM fields, nursing, arts, cybersecurity, business, and many more. Enjoy apartment-style residences, nationally ranked dining facilities, and supportive campus life just north of Atlanta. Ready for your career dreams to become a reality? Get Info Apply Now

sources.Title: Graduate Admissions
sources.Text: You’ve got a big decision to make. See for yourself why KSU is the ideal choice. Our in-person campus tours are designed specifically for students and their families. Led by a tour guide, the campus tour highlights academic programs, housing, dining and admissions. You can choose to visit both the Kennesaw and Marietta campuses or the campus that houses your area of academic interest. Take a Virtual Tour Apply Now
